verification_notes: Course opened 2022 — confirmed by multiple sources. Designer Toby Cobb confirmed by golfbangkok.com and golftripz.com. Fees are all-inclusive packages; no itemized breakdown available online. Package pricing from THB 6,500/golfer on weekdays (2-person group) with THB 1,000 weekend surcharge, confirmed by taigolf.com. GPS coordinates confirmed by golfbangkok.com course listing. No major disputes. Marked provisional because fee data comes from a single primary source (taigolf.com) for the all-inclusive amount.

designer.value: Toby Cobb (design associate of Coore & Crenshaw)

prose.overview: Siam Country Club Bangkok is the newest and most ambitious addition to the Siam Country Club portfolio, Thailand's most respected golf brand. Opened in 2022 on the Bangna-Trad Highway in Samut Prakan, it was designed by Toby Cobb — a close design associate of Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw — bringing a decidedly American architectural philosophy to Bangkok's southern suburbs. The result is a hybrid layout that marries a sparsely treed parkland feel with genuine links characteristics: wide, flowing corridors, naturalistic bunkering, and a course that rewards strategic positioning over sheer power. At 7,250 yards from the tips with Zoysia fairways and ultra-dwarf Bermuda greens, it sets a high bar for conditioning. With only four water holes, it is unusually benign for a Bangkok area course and suits golfers who prefer to think their way around rather than manage constant water carries. The club's premium branding means fees sit at the top of the Bangkok market.
prose.layout_and_experience: Siam Country Club Bangkok occupies gently undulating land along the Bangna-Trad corridor and delivers a course that feels completely different from the flat, water-heavy tracks that define most Bangkok-area golf. Toby Cobb's design draws inspiration from the American Southwest and Scottish links traditions: fairways are generous in width but cleverly contoured so that the ideal angle of approach is never obvious. Bunkering is strategic rather than punitive, placed to frame landing zones and protect greens from run-up approaches. Greens are large, undulating, and capable of holding a well-struck iron while frustrating anything off-line. Multiple tee options allow golfers of all levels to enjoy the course — from the 7,250-yard championship tees to the 5,291-yard forward tees. The relative scarcity of water hazards compared to Bangkok norms means the challenge comes from ground-game management, especially when courses firm up in the dry season. There are no signature 'postcard' holes in the classical sense; instead, the course reveals itself gradually across 18 holes, each with its own spatial character. The conditioning is consistently premier, benefiting from the resources and standards of the wider Siam Country Club organisation. The course is closed on Mondays for maintenance, and buggy usage is compulsory — appropriate given the scale of the layout.
prose.tips: Book well in advance, particularly for weekday rounds as the course is relatively new and attracting significant interest from both local and visiting golfers. Fees are all-inclusive (green fee, caddie, cart) and positioned at the premium end of the Bangkok market, so budget accordingly — weekday packages from around THB 6,500 per person, with a THB 1,000 surcharge at weekends and on public holidays. Dress code is strictly enforced: collared shirts, appropriate golf trousers or shorts. The course is located approximately 40 minutes from the Sukhumvit hotel district — arrange transport in advance or use the club's transfer service. The course is situated near Suvarnabhumi Airport, making it convenient for an arrival or departure-day round. Given the firmness of the course design, a low-trajectory game is advantageous in the windy dry-season months (November to February). Caddies are knowledgeable and tip-worthy.
prose.location_and_access: Siam Country Club Bangkok sits on the 22nd kilometre of the Bangna-Trad Highway in Samut Prakan province, approximately 35 km south-east of central Bangkok. The journey from the Sukhumvit hotel corridor takes around 40 minutes off-peak. The location also places it roughly 20 minutes from Suvarnabhumi International Airport, making it particularly appealing for golfers with flexible departure times. There is no direct public transport; a taxi, Grab, or club transfer service is recommended. The address is 111 Moo 2, Bang Na-Trat Frontage Road, Samut Prakan 10570.
prose.rental_cta_context: If you are flying into Bangkok and want to play Siam Country Club Bangkok without lugging clubs through the airport, LENGOLF delivers tour-quality rental sets directly to your hotel — so you arrive ready to play.
